candidates.title: Japanese Tech Giants Form Joint Venture for Domestic AI Development
candidates.description: SoftBank, Sony, NEC, and Honda announced the formation of a new company aimed at developing high-performance, Japanese-made AI, with a particular focus on 'physical AI' for robotics and automotive control. This strategic move intends to reduce Japan's reliance on foreign AI models.

candidates.title: OpenAI Releases 'Industrial Policy for the Intelligence Age' Paper
candidates.description: OpenAI published a 13-page policy paper titled 'Industrial Policy for the Intelligence Age,' advocating for a new 'social contract' and 'people-first' approach to AI development. The release coincided with the opening of a new Washington D.C. office dedicated to collaboration with non-profits and policymakers.

candidates.title: U.S. Air Force Seeks Proposals for Massive AI Data Centers in Alaska
candidates.description: The Department of the Air Force issued a request for proposals from AI firms to construct up to 12 large-scale data centers across three bases in Alaska. This initiative is aimed at bolstering national AI infrastructure and capabilities for defense applications.

candidates.title: Maine and Missouri Advance Bills to Ban AI Therapy Chatbots in Clinical Use
candidates.description: Legislatures in Maine and Missouri progressed with bills that would prohibit the use of AI therapy chatbots for clinical applications. These actions reflect growing concerns over the safety and efficacy of AI in sensitive healthcare sectors.
